description:
## Goal

Make cross-process send share, rather than copy, the bytes behind a ProcBin. The sender's ProcBin and the receiver's ProcBin both reference the same SharedBin; the refcount tracks the number of live references.

## Depends on

fz-cty.3 (ProcBin + MSO + sweep).

## Changes

### runtime/src/heap.rs — deep_copy_value
Add a match arm for `HeapKind::ProcBin`:

```rust
HeapKind::ProcBin => {
    let shared_ptr = unsafe { std::ptr::read((sp as *const u8).add(16) as *const *mut SharedBin) };
    unsafe { shared_bin_retain(shared_ptr); }
    let new_p = dst_heap.alloc_procbin(shared_ptr);
    forwarding.insert(sp, new_p);
    return FzValue(new_p as u64);
}
```

Notes:
- `dst_heap.alloc_procbin` pushes the new ProcBin onto the dst heap's mso_list as part of allocation; no extra MSO bookkeeping needed here.
- The forwarding map (which prevents double-copying shared structure within a single send) ensures that if the same src ProcBin appears multiple times in the value graph, only one dst ProcBin is allocated and only one retain happens.

## Tests

- Single-process round-trip: allocate a ProcBin in heap A, deep_copy to heap B. Assert: heap A's MSO still has the entry; heap B's MSO has one entry; SharedBin refcount = 2; bytes accessible from both ProcBins.
- Shared structure: build a value graph where a Tuple contains the same ProcBin pointer twice. deep_copy. Assert: dst contains exactly one new ProcBin (forwarding-map deduplication); SharedBin refcount = 2 (not 3).
- Refcount drop on heap destruction: drop heap B; assert refcount returns to 1; heap A unaffected.
- Mailbox delivery: simulate full send-via-runtime path (covered by existing test infrastructure if available); assert MSO entries are correctly placed in receiver.

## Non-changes

- Bitstring allocation policy (.5 handles that).
- ProcBin construction is not yet user-reachable — there is no caller that produces a ProcBin until .5 wires the policy into `alloc_bitstring`.

## Verification

- New tests pass.
- Refcount invariant holds across deep_copy and subsequent drops.
title: fz-shr.4: deep_copy_value handles ProcBin via retain
